channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa faces worst-ever foot-and-mouth disease outbreak due to neglected veterinary investment
item.description: South Africa is experiencing its most severe foot-and-mouth disease (FMD) outbreak in history, devastating the cattle farming sector. Experts attribute the crisis to long-term underinvestment in veter...

item.title: Military presence in Mexico, Bolivian Carnival celebrations, and unique gatherings in Buenos Aires captured in photos
item.description: Over the past week, Latin America and the Caribbean have witnessed a mix of events captured vividly by photojournalists. In Mexico, soldiers were deployed to several states, including Michoacán and Ja...

item.title: Geological evidence suggests a significant earthquake occurred near the time of Jesus's crucifixion
item.description: A study conducted in 2012, recently resurfacing online, suggests that a significant earthquake may have occurred around the time of Jesus's crucifixion, as described in the Gospel of Matthew. Research...

item.title: Young TikTok Designer Alexei Hamblin Hired to Revamp Legacy Sports Brand Slazenger
item.description: 23-year-old designer and TikTok content creator Alexei Hamblin launched a video series in 2025 critiquing and proposing revamps for outdated sports brands sold at Sports Direct. His ideas, which gaine...

item.title: Nvidia Reports Record Revenue Amid Growing AI Demand
item.description: Nvidia reported a record fourth-quarter revenue of $68.1 billion, marking a 73% increase compared to the same period last year. This surpasses analyst expectations despite widespread investor skeptici...

item.title: Fiji faces a combined crisis of drugs, HIV, and gang-related violence
item.description: Fiji is grappling with a complex public health and social crisis as the country confronts simultaneous challenges from drug abuse, the spread of HIV, and rising gang activity. Authorities have reporte...

item.title: Court Rejects Legal Challenge Against VAT on Private School Fees
item.description: A group of private schools has lost its legal challenge against the government's decision to remove the VAT exemption on their fees, after the Court of Appeal ruled against them. The case was argued b...

item.title: Ukrainian Families Challenge UK Asylum Rejections Over Claims Parts of Ukraine Are Safe
item.description: Several Ukrainian families have been denied asylum in the United Kingdom after the Home Office determined that they could safely relocate to western regions of Ukraine or to Kyiv. The decisions come f...

item.title: Starmer faces backlash after Labour's historic loss to the Greens in Gorton by-election
item.description: Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer has vowed to continue his leadership despite a significant setback in the Gorton and Denton by-election, where the Greens made history with their first-ever by-election ...

item.title: Yungblud Relocates BludFest to Czech Republic for 2026, Dividing Fan Opinions on Accessibility
item.description: Rock musician Yungblud, fresh off his Grammy win for Best Rock Performance with a live rendition of Black Sabbath's 'Changes,' has announced that the third edition of his self-curated festival, BludFe...

item.title: Cuban border guards fatally shoot four on US-registered speedboat after reported gunfire
item.description: Cuban authorities reported that four individuals on a US-registered speedboat were killed by border guards after allegedly opening fire on a Cuban coast guard vessel near Cayo Falcones, Villa Clara pr...

item.title: Thief steals laptop in Peterborough shop using distraction tactic
item.description: A man was caught on CCTV stealing a laptop worth £400 from the Holiday Money Exchange in Westgate Arcade, Peterborough, last Friday at around 10:40am. The thief, whose face was concealed by a cap, exe...

item.title: Sinkhole Opens at Omaha Intersection, Swallowing Two Vehicles
item.description: A sudden sinkhole opened at a busy intersection in Omaha, Nebraska, on 24 February, swallowing two vehicles and temporarily trapping their drivers. CCTV footage released by the Omaha Police Department...

item.title: US Energy Storage Sees Record Growth Amid Rising Grid Demand
item.description: In 2025, the United States experienced unprecedented growth in energy storage, adding 57 gigawatt hours of new battery capacity according to the Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA). This repres...

item.title: Gorton and Denton byelection sees strong turnout as Greens make significant gains
item.description: The Gorton and Denton byelection has seen an unusually high voter turnout of over 47%, with 36,903 votes verified. The contest has drawn national attention due to the challenge posed by the Green part...

item.title: The Growing Trend of Children Doing Strength Training: Safety and Benefits
item.description: The article explores the increasing number of children engaging in strength training, often with their parents' encouragement. Personal trainer Eve Stevenson, who is also a former British weightliftin...

item.title: Three-Year-Old Walks Following Pioneering In-Utero Stem Cell Surgery for Spina Bifida
item.description: Tobi Maginnis, a three-year-old boy diagnosed with severe spina bifida, has defied expectations and is able to walk after undergoing a pioneering stem cell procedure while still in the womb. Spina bif...

item.title: UK Defence Secretary orders review of RAF records for links to Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: The UK Defence Secretary, John Healey, has instructed officials to examine over two decades of Ministry of Defence records to determine whether the late convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein utilised R...

item.title: Brady Tkachuk Denies Involvement in Fake TikTok Video Shared by White House
item.description: Brady Tkachuk, the captain of the NHL's Ottawa Senators and a member of Team USA, has publicly refuted claims that he made derogatory comments about Canadians in a TikTok video shared by the White Hou...

item.title: Amanda Knox launches podcast examining Lucy Letby case
item.description: Amanda Knox, the American woman famously acquitted of the murder of Meredith Kercher in Italy, has launched a podcast series titled 'Doubt: The Case of Lucy Letby'. The series aims to scrutinise the c...
